ground_truth.title: Top 10 Appliance Manufacturers in the U.S.A. - IndustrySelect®
ground_truth.author: IndustrySelect
ground_truth.main_content: Homes and businesses cannot function without modern appliances. The U.S. appliance manufacturing sector includes refrigerators and freezers, washers and dryers, cooking appliances, and heating and cooling appliances. While the increase in the sector's jobs over the last year was modest at 0.99%, you can expect emerging intelligent technologies and increased consumer purchasing power to drive further growth. While statistics such as women and minority ownership, at 2% and 1%, respectively, mirror those of total manufacturing, the appliance manufacturing sector departs in several significant areas. The sector imports 24% of its raw materials versus 11% for manufacturing in general. International distribution at 52% versus 29% for total manufacturing goes a long way to compensate. Domestic distribution of appliances is only 25%, while for manufacturing as a whole, it stands at 70%. Appliance manufacturers have 13% public ownership, while total manufacturing has only 5%. Most of the 383 appliance manufacturers in the U.S. (89 or 23%) are in the Midwest. The second largest cluster (57 or 15%) locate their facilities in the South. The West houses 37 or 10% and the Northeast 25 or 7%. Each of the top 10 appliance manufacturers employs over 1000 workers. Since only one of these companies is located outside the Midwest or the South, you may find those regions your most promising prospecting zones. Haier U.S. Appliance Solutions, Inc. is the new name for G.E. Appliances, headquartered in Louisville, Kentucky. The company embraces the Internet of Things (IoT) to produce intelligent appliances and drive industry growth. It provides 6,000 jobs. Whirlpool Corp. in Clyde, Ohio, employs 3,500 workers. The facility produces washers and dryers under Kenmore, KitchenAid, Roper, and Estate brands. Electrolux was originally famous for vacuum cleaners, which it still manufactures, and it expanded into other appliances. Its washer and dryer are the best-reviewed of 2023. The Springfield, Tennessee, facility has a workforce of 2,800. Marion, Ohio, prides itself that its Whirlpool facility is the world's largest clothes dryer manufacturing plant. Whirlpool uses 2,500 workers to turn the dryers out. The Whirlpool Corporation in Findlay, Ohio, employs 2,300 workers. This facility uses wind power to offset 22% of its energy demand. Roper Corporation in La Fayette, Georgia, claims to be the home of America's best cooking products. The 2000 employees manufacture G.E., Monogram, CAFÉ, Profile, and Hotpoint appliances. Philips Oral Healthcare in Bothell, Washington, sells Sonicare toothbrushes and other products to dental professionals. The company employs 1800 people. The 1,400 workers at A. O. Smith Water Products Co. in Ashland City, Tennessee, make water heaters and water treatment products. The 1,300 workers at Whirlpool in Cleveland, Tennessee, make significant appliances for the wholesale market. Bradford White's 1,200 employees in Middleville, MI, make a line of water heaters and boilers. They produce tank and tankless models for both residential and commercial use.
